secondary_sources.name: HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard v3
secondary_sources.use: Open-weight historical baseline; dataset open-llm-leaderboard/contents (4580 models, 6 benchmarks)

secondary_sources.name: Public benchmark consensus (2026 community)
secondary_sources.use: MMLU/HellaSwag/GSM8K saturation status from survey arxiv 2508.15361 + multiple leaderboards

classification_rule.near_saturated: top-3 spread ≤ 5pp AND mean(top-3) ≥ 80% (and not saturated)
classification_rule.sparse_data: fewer than 3 frontier models report this benchmark publicly

honest_disclaimer: Threshold-sensitive: a benchmark with spread 5.0pp classifies as near_saturated; 5.1pp classifies as discriminative. Verdicts within ±1pp of cutoffs should be read as 'borderline'. Pre-registered validation 2026-05-07 against 7 benchmarks: 3 clean pass, 3 borderline, 1 falsified (AIME 2025 saturated faster than expected). Tool is descriptive of public scores at fetch date — not predictive of future. Always cross-check before strategic decisions.

MMLU.description: Massive Multitask Language Understanding — 57 subjects, 4-way multiple choice.

top_3.model: (other frontier models no longer report — DemandSphere lists N/A)

MMLU.classification_basis: consensus + DemandSphere stopped tracking N/A for many frontier models, plus survey 2508.15361 confirms 88-94% top range cluster.
MMLU.note: MMLU was useful 2020-2023. By 2024 it stopped discriminating frontier models. Many leaderboards have dropped it. Use MMLU-Pro for general 7B-70B; GPQA/HLE for frontier.

MMLU-Pro.description: Harder, 10-way multiple choice version of MMLU. Reasoning-heavier.
MMLU-Pro.note: Approximate top-3 — not in DemandSphere primary set. Confirm via HF Open LLM Leaderboard v3 for current numbers.

GPQA-Diamond.description: Graduate-level Google-Proof Q&A — PhD-level science questions designed to be uncontaminable by web search.
GPQA-Diamond.classification_basis: spread 5.8pp > 5pp cutoff. Borderline near-saturated. Trend: tightening; will likely cross into near-saturated in 2026 H2.
GPQA-Diamond.note: Borderline. Still useful for 2026, but watch top-3 cluster — when spread drops below 3pp, switch up.

SWE-bench-Verified.description: Real GitHub issues + tests. Agent must produce a patch that passes the project's tests.
SWE-bench-Verified.classification_basis: spread 9.9pp >> 5pp. Strong differentiation between top-2 and rank-3.
SWE-bench-Verified.note: Vendor self-report risk: many SWE-bench scores are vendor-published; cross-check with [SWE-bench Verified leaderboard](https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/swe-bench-verified) which lists 89 self-reported vs 0 verified.

HumanEval.description: 164 Python programming problems with unit tests. Standard since 2021.
HumanEval.note: HumanEval has been considered effectively saturated since 2024. Heavy contamination risk: original problems are in many crawls.

LiveCodeBench-Pro.description: Continuously refreshed competitive programming problems — designed against contamination.
LiveCodeBench-Pro.classification_basis: Only 2 frontier models report in DemandSphere set. More data needed.
recommendations: Use this benchmark; few alternatives at this contamination resistance
LiveCodeBench-Pro.note: Top-1 (DeepSeek V4 Pro 93.5) suspiciously high vs rank-2 (76.0) — possible chat-template or test-set version mismatch. Verify reproducibility before quoting.

MATH.description: Competition-style math problems (algebra, geometry, prealgebra, etc).
MATH.classification_basis: spread 0.1pp ≤ 2pp, mean 97.3 ≥ 90%. Reasoning models cluster at top.
MATH.note: MATH was the harder math benchmark for years. Saturated by reasoning models in 2024-2025.

AIME-2025.description: American Invitational Math Examination 2025 problems — 15 short-answer integer problems.
AIME-2025.classification_basis: spread 3.9pp ≤ 5pp, mean 98.6 ≥ 90%. Frontier perfect or near-perfect.
AIME-2025.note: 🚨 AIME 2025 saturated FASTER than expected (within ~6 months of release). This was the falsified pre-registered case in 2026-05-07 validation. Use as cautionary tale for any newly-released math bench.

HLE.description: Humanity's Last Exam — designed by Center for AI Safety + Scale AI as the hardest standardized eval. Multidomain, expert-curated.
HLE.classification_basis: spread 12.5pp >> 5pp, mean 43.0 << 80%. Top model only at 50% — large headroom.
recommendations: HLE is currently the strongest frontier discriminator. Use it.
HLE.note: Released specifically to address saturation. As of 2026-05, no model has cracked 60%. Expected useful lifespan: 2-3 years.

ARC-AGI-2.description: Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus v2 — visual pattern puzzles designed against pretraining contamination.
ARC-AGI-2.classification_basis: Mean << 80%. Massive headroom. (Approximate — not in DemandSphere primary set.)
recommendations: ARC-AGI 2 itself remains discriminative for years to come.
ARC-AGI-2.note: Approximate scores — confirm via official ARC-AGI leaderboard. Tool should display 'approximate' badge.

HellaSwag.classification_basis: Saturated since 2023; most frontier leaderboards have dropped tracking it.
HellaSwag.note: Useful only as a regression check on small models (<3B).

GSM8K.description: Grade-school word problems requiring multi-step arithmetic.
GSM8K.classification_basis: Most frontier models 95%+ since 2024. Heavy contamination evidence.
GSM8K.note: Contaminated. Top scores reflect memorization more than reasoning.

MMMU.description: Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding & Reasoning — expert-level cross-domain VLM benchmark.
MMMU.classification_basis: spread 0.6pp ≤ 2pp; mean 85.6% < 90% but ≥ 80% → near_saturated. Approaching human expert 88.6% (Apr 2026 data).
MMMU.note: VLM-only. Source: llm-stats.com MMMU leaderboard 2026-04. Top models within 0.3pp of human expert performance — expect full saturation by 2026-Q4.

MMMU-Pro.description: Harder MMMU variant with 10-way MC + augmented questions to resist contamination.
MMMU-Pro.classification_basis: spread 10.1pp >> 5pp → discriminative. NOTE: alternate llm-stats reading (GPT-5.5 88.3 / Gemini 3.1 Pro 88.2 / Gemini 3 Flash 87.6) gives spread 0.7pp → near_saturated. Methodology variance is real.
MMMU-Pro.note: Two public leaderboards disagree by ~6pp on top model — chat-template / sampling differences likely. Cross-check before quoting.

VisScience.description: K-12 science multimodal benchmark — 3000 questions across math, physics, chemistry; 5 difficulty levels.
VisScience.classification_basis: spread 15.2pp >> 5pp; mean 46.2% << 80%. Massive headroom; subject-specific leaders differ. 25 MLLMs evaluated.
recommendations: VisScience itself remains discriminative for years.
VisScience.note: Top-3 here picks BEST model per subject (math/chem/physics). Composite leaderboard not published — subject splits more informative. Closed-source MLLMs generally outperform open-source.
